The '''62nd''' annual '''[[Berlin International Film Festival]]''' was held from 9 to 19 February 2012. British film director [[Mike Leigh]] was the president of the jury.<ref name="Yahoo">{{cite web |url=https://news.yahoo.com/uk-director-mike-leigh-head-berlin-film-jury-141842169.html |title=UK director Mike Leigh to head Berlin film jury |access-date=11 December 2011 |work=Yahoo}}</ref> The first five films to be screened in the competition were announced on 19 December 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2012/01_jahresblatt_2012/01_jahresblatt_2012.html|title=62ND BERLIN INTERNATIONAL FILM FESTIVAL FEBRUARY 09 - 19, 2012 |date=19 December 2011|access-date=19 December 2011|work=berlinale.de}}</ref> American actress [[Meryl Streep]] was presented with the [[Golden Bear#Golden Bear .E2.80.93 Honorary Award|Honorary Golden Bear]] on 14 February.<ref name="Streep">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-16389439|title=Berlin Film Festival to honour Meryl Streep |date=4 January 2012|access-date=4 January 2012|work=BBC}}</ref> [[Benoît Jacquot]]'s film ''[[Farewell, My Queen]]'' was announced as the opening film.<ref name="Opening">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-16422935|title=Marie Antoinette drama to open Berlin Film Festival |date=5 January 2012|access-date=5 January 2012|work=BBC}}</ref>

The [[Golden Bear]] went to the Italian film ''[[Caesar Must Die]]'', directed by [[Paolo and Vittorio Taviani]], which also served as closing night film.<ref name="BBC Berlin">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-17085227 |title=Jail docu-drama Caesar Must Die wins Berlin award |date=19 February 2012 |access-date=19 February 2012 |work=BBC News}}</ref>

==Juries==
The following people were announced as being on the jury for the festival:<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2012/04_jury_2012/04_jury_2012.html |title=JURIES 2012 |access-date=10 June 2014 |work=berlinale.de | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20130929165725/http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2012/04_jury_2012/04_jury_2012.html| archive-date=29 September 2013 | url-status= live}}</ref>

=== Main Competition ===
* [[Mike Leigh]], British filmmaker - Jury President
* [[Anton Corbijn]], Dutch photographer and director
* [[Asghar Farhadi]], Iranian filmmaker and producer
* [[Charlotte Gainsbourg]], French actress
* [[Jake Gyllenhaal]], American actor
* [[François Ozon]], French filmmaker
* [[Boualem Sansal]], Algerian writer
* [[Barbara Sukowa]], German actress

=== Best First Feature Award ===
* [[Matthew Modine]], American actor
* Hania Mroué, Lebanese producer and distributor
* Moritz Rinke, German journalist and writer

=== Short Film Competition ===
* [[Sandra Hüller]], German actress
* [[Emily Jacir]],Palestine artist and producer
* [[David OReilly (artist)|David O'Reilly]], Irish director, screenwriter and producer

== Official Awards ==

=== Main Competition ===
[[File:Paolo Taviani and Vittorio Storaro.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Paolo and Vittorio Taviani]], winners of the Golden Bear at the festival]]
The following prizes were awarded by the International Jury:<ref name="BerlinPrizes">{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2012/03_preistrger_2012/03_preistraeger_2012.html |title=Prizes of the International Jury 2012 |date=19 February 2012 |access-date=19 February 2012 |work=Berlinale}}</ref> ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'' described the outcome as "a major upset". ''[[Der Spiegel]]'' said it was a "very conservative selection."<ref name="BBC News" /> ''[[Der Tagesspiegel]]'' criticised the outcome, "The jury shunned almost all the contemporary films that were admired or hotly debated at an otherwise pretty remarkable festival."<ref name="Expatica">{{cite web|url=http://www.expatica.com/de/news/local_news/critics-lament-conservative-winner-at-edgy-berlin-film-fest_209371.html|title=Critics lament conservative winner at edgy Berlin film fest|date=19 February 2012|access-date=19 February 2012|work=Expatica|archive-date=28 November 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121128074804/http://www.expatica.com/de/news/local_news/critics-lament-conservative-winner-at-edgy-berlin-film-fest_209371.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>  Paolo Taviani said "We hope that when the film is released to the general public that cinemagoers will say to themselves or even those around them... that even a prisoner with a dreadful sentence, even a life sentence, is and remains a human being". Vittorio Taviani read out the names of the cast.<ref name="BBC News">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-17085227|title=Jail docu-drama Caesar Must Die wins Berlin award|date=19 February 2012|access-date=19 February 2012|work=BBC News}}</ref>

* [[Golden Bear]]: ''[[Caesar Must Die]]'' by [[Paolo and Vittorio Taviani]]
* [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]]: ''[[Just the Wind]]'' by [[Benedek Fliegauf]]
* [[Silver Bear for Best Director]]: [[Christian Petzold (director)|Christian Petzold]] for ''[[Barbara (2012 film)|Barbara]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actress]]: Rachel Mwanza for ''[[War Witch]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actor]]: [[Mikkel Boe Følsgaard]] for ''[[A Royal Affair]]''
* '''Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Contribution''': Lutz Reitemeier for ''[[White Deer Plain (film)|White Deer Plain]]'' (cinematography)
* [[Silver Bear for Best Screenplay]]: [[Nikolaj Arcel]] and Rasmus Heisterberg for ''[[A Royal Affair]]''
* [[Alfred Bauer Prize]]: ''[[Tabu (2012 film)|Tabu]]'' by Miguel Gomes
* Special Award – Silver Bear: ''[[Sister (2012 film)|Sister]]'' by [[Ursula Meier]]

=== [[Golden Bear#Golden Bear – Honorary Award|Honorary Golden Bear]] ===
* [[Meryl Streep]]

=== Crystal Bears ===
* Generation Kplus (Feature Length Film): ''[[Arcadia (2012 film)|Arcadia]]'' by Olivia Silver
* Generation 14plus (Feature Length Film): ''Night of Silence'' by Reis Çelik

=== [[International Federation of Film Critics|FIPRESCI]] Prizes ===
*Competition: ''[[Tabu (2012 film)|Tabu]]'' by Miguel Gomes
*Panorama: ''Atomic Age'' by Héléna Klotz
*Forum: ''Hemel'' by [[Sacha Polak]]

=== Golden Bear for Best Short Film ===

* ''Rafa'' by [[João Salaviza]]
